sourceMovie.title: The Fault in Our Stars
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Fault in Our Stars (2014) as a romance theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Me Before You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Fault in Our Stars clicked for you, Me Before You (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Brokeback Mountain
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Fault in Our Stars clicked for you, Brokeback Mountain (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Fault in Our Stars clicked for you, Love, Rosie (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Fault in Our Stars?
faq.answer: Try Me Before You, Brokeback Mountain, Love, Rosie—each shares DNA with The Fault in Our Stars while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Fault in Our Stars?
faq.answer: The Fault in Our Stars (2014) is best described as Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Fault in Our Stars worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
